intro:

Annona candida is a tropical evergreen tree that belongs to the custard apple family (Annonaceae). This plant is known for its edible fruit and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Annona candida include annonin, a toxic compound that has been shown to have antiproliferative and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Annona candida include fruit extracts, teas, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
